soap:
SOAP lance-format/lance#7463 — feat(index): read a column's min/max from ZoneMap without a scan

RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7463
reviewed 1fc46c07 → 855f0738 (1 commit, 3 files; 2 base-merge files excluded from delta)
prior review: wjones127 APPROVED @ 1fc46c07, with one inline **nitpick** (non-blocking)
CI: green · mergeable: true

---

SECTION A — CONDITIONS LEDGER (1/1 addressed; 0 blocking)

The packet reconstructed 0 conditions because my prior ledger held no `issue(blocking)` comments — my last review was an approval carrying a single **nitpick** (conventional label, explicitly non-blocking). I still verify it against the delta patch rather than the resolved flag.

~ met (nitpick, non-blocking) — "I don't love adding more methods to Dataset. It would be nice if there was some sort of `Dataset::statistics()` handle this was applied to, rather than adding a method to a top-level object."
  → Verified against delta, not the thread. At 1fc46c07 `column_value_range` was an `async fn` on `Dataset` via the `DatasetIndexExt` trait in `rust/lance/src/index.rs:1458` (exactly where I anchored the nitpick). The delta introduces `Dataset::statistics() -> DatasetStatistics<'_>` (`rust/lance/src/dataset.rs:+457`) and relocates the method onto the new `DatasetStatistics<'a>` wrapper struct (`rust/lance/src/dataset/statistics.rs:+95..+186`). Call sites migrated `ds.column_value_range(x)` → `ds.statistics().column_value_range(x)` (`rust/lance/src/index/scalar.rs`, 4 test sites). This is precisely the `Dataset::statistics()` handle I asked for — condition addressed.
  → Regression check on the move: I diffed the old body against the new one. It is a verbatim relocation — the only textual change is `self.` → `dataset.` (with `dataset = self.dataset`). Segment matching, joint-coverage subset check, opened-index lifetime anchoring, downcast, and the fold are byte-for-byte identical. No behavior changed in the move.

No blocking conditions were ever outstanding; nothing to carry forward.

---

SECTION B — FRESH-EYES DELTA

Independent read of everything that changed since 1fc46c07. The delta is a pure refactor (relocation) plus its test-call migration; the ZoneMap read-path logic in `value_range_over` is unchanged by this commit. I re-audited the read-path correctness against upstream source at head (`rust/lance-index/src/scalar/zonemap.rs@855f0738`) since correctness of the fast path is the review's charter.

Read-path correctness (verified against ground truth, all sound):

- Null handling — `scalar_is_finite_bound` (`zonemap.rs:192`) excludes `is_null()` mins/maxes from the fold; an all-null zone contributes no bound. If every zone lacks a finite bound, `min?`/`max?` (`zonemap.rs:187`) short-circuits to `None`. Correct — a column with only nulls yields `None`, not a spurious range.
- NaN handling — any zone whose `max` is NaN aborts the whole fold with `None` (`zonemap.rs:173-175`). The doc reasoning is right: `ScalarValue`'s total order ranks NaN above all finite values, so a NaN-bearing zone hides its true finite max; folding only finite maxes would produce a *subset* upper bound that could prune live rows. Returning `None` is the sound conservative choice.
- Empty / no-finite-bound zones — handled by the `min?`/`max?` short-circuit; no panic, no partial range.
- Type boundaries — the fold compares via `partial_cmp(...).is_some_and(is_lt/is_gt)`; incomparable pairs don't update the bound. For finite non-NaN scalars of a single column's type this is well-defined. Bounds cloned out (`min?.clone()`), no lifetime hazard.
- Fast-path vs full-scan agreement — soundness rests on the caller's joint-coverage gate: `dataset.fragment_bitmap.is_subset(&covered)` where `covered` is the union of segment `fragment_bitmap`s (`statistics.rs:+147..+152`). If any segment lacks a `fragment_bitmap`, or live fragments aren't fully covered (e.g. a fragment appended after index build), it returns `None`. Result is a *superset* of live values, conservative under deletion vectors — safe to prune with, never tighter than truth. Migrated tests exercise exactly this: global `[0,9]` fold, `None` after appending an uncovered fragment, and multi-segment fold across two index segments. All three still pass through the new `ds.statistics()` entry point.

New concerns introduced by the delta: none.

Minor, non-blocking observations:

- nitpick: `DatasetStatistics<'a>` derives `Copy` while holding `&'a Dataset` — fine today, but if this handle later grows owned state the `Copy` will have to be removed. Optional note; no action now.
- praise: the doc comment on `column_value_range` correctly propagates the soundness contract (superset, conservative under deletes, `None` on NaN / partial coverage) to the public surface.

Coverage note (informational): codecov reports 94% patch coverage with 14 uncovered lines; the uncovered lines are error/early-return arms (field not found, missing `fragment_bitmap`, downcast failure). Happy path, append-uncovered→`None`, and multi-segment fold all covered. Not blocking.

---

RECOMMENDATION: approve
  The one prior nitpick is addressed by exactly the `Dataset::statistics()` handle I suggested; the move is a verbatim, behavior-preserving relocation; the ZoneMap min/max read path (null / NaN / empty-zone / coverage-gate) is sound and unchanged; CI green, mergeable.
  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if the 2 files excluded from the delta (base-branch merges, not the PR's own work) actually contained PR-authored changes to the read path or its callers — scoped to the author's own diff per the skill, did not audit those. Also assumes `ScalarValue::is_null()` returns true for a fully-null zone summary min/max, not re-derived from the trainer.
